texto: Far too long hath there been a slave and a tyrant concealed in a woman. On that account woman is not yet capable of friendship: she knoweth only love. In a woman's love there is injustice and blindness to all she doth not love. And even in woman's conscious love, there is always surprise and lightening and night , along with the light.
explicacao: Citação de Friedrich Neitzsche sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Far too long hath there been a slave and a tyrant concealed in a woman. On that account woman is …” articula amizade, neitzsche, women em registro de amizade, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: Far too long hath there been a slave and a tyrant concealed in a woman. On that account woman is not yet capable of friendship: she knoweth only love. In a woman's love there is injustice and blindness to all she doth not love. And even in woman's conscious love, there is always surprise and lightening and night , along with the light.

texto: Are designations congruent with things? Is language the adequate expression of all realities?It is only by means of forgetfulness that man can ever reach the point of fancying himself to possess a "truth" of the grade just indicated. If he will not be satisfied with truth in the form of tautology, that is to say, if he will not be content with empty husks, then he will always exchange truths for illusions.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Friedrich Neitzsche que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Are designations congruent with things? Is language the adequate expression of all realities?It i…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, denial, falsity, com foco em mudanca e falsity.
